mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in UNRATE below 4.0% signals rapid labor market cooling that reduces wage inflation pressures, prompting markets to price in imminent rate cuts and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses below 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in RSAFS and HOUST indicates collapsing consumer spending and housing demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 50% during co-contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding INDPRO during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient production capacity that outpaces demand destruction, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS rises, with negative return frequency exceeding 55% in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level exceeding 2.5% triggers immediate repricing of growth equity valuations due to higher borrowing costs, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 5-day returns in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 2.5%, invalidating the discount rate compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in PAYEMS expands household income and consumer purchasing power, which directly boosts corporate sales forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases consistently precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns, with a negative return frequency above 55% across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising CPIAUCSL and falling GDPC1 indicates stagflationary pressure that erodes real consumer spending and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-GDPC1 divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive returns occurring in >60% of stagflationary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid supply chain normalization that eliminates input cost bottlenecks, triggering positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion during recovery phases yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.
